sources.title: Google Scholar: Top AI/ML Venues by Citations
Semantic Scholar: Most Influential ML Papers 2023-2024
Papers With Code: Top Papers of 2023

result.summary: The most cited ML papers of 2023-2024 are dominated by LLM scaling and alignment research, with Llama 2, Mistral 7B, and DPO (Direct Preference Optimization) each accumulating 2,000+ citations within months of publication, signaling a field-wide focus on accessible and alignable language models.
key_findings: Llama 2 (Touvron et al., 2023) leads with 5,800+ citations in 12 months, driven by open-weight release enabling thousands of downstream research projects and fine-tuned variants

DPO: Direct Preference Optimization (Rafailov et al., 2023) accumulated 2,400+ citations by simplifying RLHF into a classification-style loss, becoming the default alignment method for open-source models
Multimodal papers gained momentum with LLaVA and GPT-4V analysis papers each exceeding 1,500 citations, indicating a shift from text-only to vision-language research
